tagline: Parasyte — A parasite takes over your right hand and becomes your unlikely partner in survival.
visualizationReason: Parasyte's Migi operates as a symbiotic peer node to Shinichi's human consciousness—sharing one body while maintaining distinct identities and abilities. The parasite control system creates affinity relationships between parasite types and host organisms, making it a natural affinity-matrix where each node has individual capabilities that combine with but remain distinct from the host.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: Migi isn't a monster. He's the only one who actually understands what's happening.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYS_WARN]: PARASITE CO-INFECTION DETECTED — HOST INTEGRITY: SHARED

introductionSummary: Parasyte follows Shinichi Izumi, a normal high schooler whose right hand is taken over by Migi—a parasite alien who represents the invasion's vanguard. Against all odds, Shinichi and Migi develop a genuine symbiotic partnership: Migi needs Shinichi's body to survive, and Shinichi needs Migi's power to fight the increasingly dangerous parasites hunting them both. The system's tension comes from the parasite-human boundary—how much of Shinichi remains Shinichi when half his body thinks for itself?

aiInsights.casual: An alien parasite takes over Shinichi's right hand and they become an unlikely team—he's got the brain, Migi's got the monster-fighting ability. It's a body-horror invasion story with surprisingly sweet moments of共生.
aiInsights.deep: Parasyte runs a split-consciousness operating model: Migi processes threat analysis and combat while Shinichi maintains social identity and decision-making authority. Their negotiation over body control creates a unique co-governance system where neither can fully override the other without destroying the host. The parasites' mindless swarm behavior versus their sophisticated individual combat abilities exposes a critical design flaw in the invasion architecture—intelligence without emotional investment produces tactical brilliance but strategic brittleness.

powerSystem.name: Parasite Biology
powerSystem.subtitle: Migi's Alien Physiology
powerSystem.loreDesc: Migi can reshape his body into any biological form—from blades and shields to eyes and weaponry—giving Shinichi access to combat capabilities no human could possess.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A shapeshifting combat node that adapts its physical structure based on threat type, operating as a biological multi-tool within the host body.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Migi extends a blade from Shinichi's arm to block a killing blow he couldn't have seen.

powerSystem.name: Symbiotic Coexistence
powerSystem.subtitle: Shared Body Governance
powerSystem.loreDesc: Shinichi and Migi must negotiate body control—Shinichi retains social autonomy while Migi handles combat response, creating a split-processing model.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A co-governance operating system where two distinct consciousness nodes share one physical substrate through negotiated time-slicing.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Migi refusing to let Shinichi sacrifice himself, insisting they survive together.

powerSystem.name: Parasite Hierarchy
powerSystem.subtitle: Mission-Designed Roles
powerSystem.loreDesc: The parasites operate with a clear division: some infiltration types blend with humans, others are combat specialists, and the core handles reproduction.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A designed-species node architecture where each parasite type has a specific functional role in the invasion network.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: The Climax reveals the true scale of the parasite reproductive strategy.

powerSystem.name: Human Emotional Investment
powerSystem.subtitle: The Symbiosis Paradox
powerSystem.loreDesc: Migi develops genuine care for Shinichi and his family—emotional attachment that no parasite should possess, making Migi a malfunctioning unit in an otherwise efficient invasion.
powerSystem.systemDesc: An emergent emotional node that breaks the parasite species' designed emotional sterility, creating loyalty outside the mission parameters.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Migi choosing to stay with Shinichi at the cost of reuniting with his own kind.
powerSystem.loreSubtitle: The parasite who learned to care

rankings.topTierLore: The central parasite intelligence directing the invasion, managing reproduction rates and combat deployment.
rankings.topTierSystem: Command layer node coordinating all parasite operations with a single coherent strategy.

tiers.name: Core Parasite
tiers.loreDesc: The central intelligence managing the invasion's reproductive and tactical decisions.
tiers.systemDesc: Command architecture node with full strategic visibility and reproduction authorization.

tiers.name: Combat Specialist
tiers.loreDesc: Parasites optimized for direct confrontation, with heavy armor and lethal weaponry.
tiers.systemDesc: High-output combat nodes deployed for target elimination.

tiers.name: Infiltrator
tiers.loreDesc: Parasites that take over human bodies and blend into society for intelligence gathering.
tiers.systemDesc: Social camouflage nodes that grant access to human networks and information.

tiers.name: Migi (Unique Co-Existor)
tiers.loreDesc: A parasite sharing a body with a human in genuine symbiosis—behaving against type.
tiers.systemDesc: An anomalous co-existence node that retains combat capability while developing emotional investment in a host.

characters.name: Shinichi Izumi
characters.title: The Parasitized Human
characters.loreBio: A high schooler whose right hand is taken over by the parasite Migi. He maintains his humanity while gaining access to combat abilities that make him humanity's only real defense.
characters.systemBio: A co-governance host node maintaining human decision authority and emotional baselines while sharing physical control with an alien combat module.
characters.signatureMoment: Choosing to save his mother from a parasite even knowing it nearly costs him Migi's protection.
characters.description: Shinichi Izumi is the parasitized protagonist of Parasyte.

abilities.name: Human Cognition
abilities.description: Full human consciousness, emotional range, and moral reasoning preserved.

abilities.name: Migi Combat Integration
abilities.description: Access to Migi's shapeshifting combat abilities while maintaining human decision authority.

abilities.name: Parasite Knowledge
abilities.description: Living with Migi gives Shinichi unique insight into parasite behavior and weaknesses.

characters.name: Migi
characters.title: The Right-Hand Parasite
characters.loreBio: A parasite who was supposed to take over Shinichi's brain but only reached his hand. Rather than a failed invasion, Migi became a genuine partner.
characters.systemBio: An adaptive combat node that achieved co-existence with a host rather than conquest, developing emotional investment that violates parasite design parameters.
characters.signatureMoment: Deciding to stay with Shinichi even when reunited parasite mass offers reunion with his own kind.
characters.description: Migi is the parasite sharing Shinichi's body in Parasyte.

abilities.name: Shapeshifting Combat
abilities.description: Can form blades, shields, spikes, and other weapons from hand tissue at will.

abilities.name: Parasite Analysis
abilities.description: Can identify other parasites and their likely attack patterns from body language.

abilities.name: Speed/Strength Boost
abilities.description: When integrated, provides superhuman physical capabilities beyond human limits.

abilities.name: Sleep Function
abilities.description: Migi's consciousness cycles through sleep states, making him unavailable for periods—but also allowing recovery.

characters.name: Gotou
characters.title: The Hybrid Human
characters.loreBio: A human whose body was fully taken over by parasites who then tried to replicate a human nervous system, resulting in a physically perfect but emotionally hollow organism.
characters.systemBio: A post-host conquest node that attempted to reverse-engineer human consciousness but only achieved behavioral mimicry without genuine emotional substrate.
characters.signatureMoment: Demonstrating his perfect human replication by fighting Shinichi and Migi while maintaining perfect calm.
characters.description: Parasite-human hybrid sent to capture Migi. Has a fully formed parasite limbd replaced his right arm. Functions as a coordinated dual-substrate combatant.

abilities.name: Full Parasite Physiology
abilities.description: Has complete parasite combat forms plus a replicated human nervous system.

abilities.name: Human Behavior Mimicry
abilities.description: Appears completely human but has no genuine emotions, making him terrifyingly consistent.

characters.name: Muramoto
characters.title: The Parasitized Father
characters.loreBio: The father of a girl in Shinichi's class who was taken over by a parasite, representing how the invasion targets families and destroys normal life.
characters.systemBio: An infiltrator node that has been deployed into a family unit, representing the parasite network's social infiltration capability.
characters.signatureMoment: Shinichi recognizing the father is a parasite from behavioral anomalies no one else notices.
characters.description: Migi-controlled corpse that Shinichi uses as a temporary body after their original host dies. Maintains parasite combat capabilities with human mobility limitations.

abilities.name: Infiltration Mimicry
abilities.description: Can maintain a convincing human appearance and social role despite being fully parasite-controlled.

relationships.loreDesc: A genuine symbiotic partnership where both have learned to care for each other's survival.
relationships.systemDesc: Co-governance node where two distinct consciousness types share one substrate through mutual necessity and emergent affection.

relationships.loreDesc: Migi cannot survive without Shinichi's body as a host, but increasingly also doesn't want to leave.
relationships.systemDesc: Host dependency edge that evolved from necessity into genuine partnership.

relationships.loreDesc: Gotou represents what Shinichi could become if parasites fully conquered humanity—perfect bodies with no souls.
relationships.systemDesc: Counter-edge representing the dystopian endpoint of failed human resistance.

relationships.loreDesc: Migi chooses Shinichi over his species, making him a defector from the parasite network.
relationships.systemDesc: Species-loyalty edge overridden by individual emotional investment.

relationships.loreDesc: Shinichi recognizes parasite-infiltrated humans by behavioral anomalies, putting him in the position of identifying and potentially eliminating them.
relationships.systemDesc: Detection edge unique to the co-existence node, making Shinichi the only human who can reliably identify infiltrators.

factions.name: Human Resistance
factions.loreDesc: Shinichi and the humans who fight back against the parasite invasion with Migi's help.
factions.systemDesc: A minimal resistance node anchored by the Shinichi-Migi co-existence unit.

factions.name: Parasite Invasion Network
factions.loreDesc: The alien parasites infiltrating and consuming human society, led by the Core.
factions.systemDesc: Coordinated invasion architecture deploying infiltrator, combat, and reproductive node types.

factions.name: Migi (Unique)
factions.loreDesc: A parasite who defected from the invasion network through emotional development.
factions.systemDesc: An anomalous node that retained combat capability while developing species-atypical loyalty to a host.

factions.name: Gotou (Hybrid)
factions.loreDesc: A full parasite takeover that attempted to replicate human neurology, representing the invasion's most sophisticated infiltration.
factions.systemDesc: Post-conquest node with maximum combat capability and social integration.

rules.name: Parasite Takes Over What It Touches
rules.subtitle: Contact initiates conversion
rules.loreConsequence: Any parasite contact with exposed flesh initiates a takeover—if Migi hadn't been interrupted, Shinichi's entire body would have been consumed.
rules.systemEquivalent: Contact-based conversion protocol where physical proximity to parasite mass equals infection risk.

rules.name: Migi Only Reached the Hand
rules.subtitle: Incomplete takeover = co-existence
rules.loreConsequence: Because Migi only reached Shinichi's hand, neither could conquer the other—creating forced symbiosis instead of possession.
rules.systemEquivalent: Partial invasion success that produces a co-governance model rather than host replacement.

rules.name: Parasites Lack Emotions
rules.subtitle: Designed species emotional sterility
rules.loreConsequence: Parasites are perfectly efficient organisms with no emotional capacity—except Migi, who develops them through prolonged human contact.
rules.systemEquivalent: Species-wide emotional sterility that creates predictable behavior, violated only by Migi's anomalous development.

rules.name: Shinichi Retains Decision Authority
rules.subtitle: Migi cannot override human will
rules.loreConsequence: Migi can refuse to act in combat, but cannot force Shinichi's body to do anything against his will.
rules.systemEquivalent: Host sovereignty constraint where the parasite module is a tool, not a controller.
rules.loreSubtitle: The human is always in charge

counterplay.mechanic: Only Shinichi can reliably identify parasite-infiltrated humans because only he knows what parasite behavior looks like from the inside.
counterplay.loreDesc: No one believes him until it's too late, making his early warnings consistently ignored.
counterplay.systemDesc: Unique detection capability arising from co-existence node architecture.

counterplay.mechanic: Migi can match Gotou's combat forms because both are adapting shapeshifters—but Shinichi's human tactical thinking gives them an edge.
counterplay.loreDesc: Gotou has the body; Shinichi and Migi have the partnership that Gotou's emptiness cannot replicate.
counterplay.systemDesc: Cooperation edge between two specialized nodes versus single perfect-form node.

counterplay.mechanic: Shinichi uses normal human life—school, family, friends—as cover that the parasites' social analysis can't penetrate.
counterplay.loreDesc: The parasites can analyze biology perfectly but can't understand why a human would care about anyone.
counterplay.systemDesc: Human emotional investment as camouflage against species designed without it.

anomalies.name: Migi's Emotional Development
anomalies.ruleViolated: Parasites should have no emotional capacity
anomalies.loreDesc: Migi develops genuine affection for Shinichi and distress at his pain—a complete violation of parasite species design.
anomalies.systemDesc: Emergent emotional node arising from sustained co-existence with human host.

anomalies.name: Shinichi's Dual-Nature Acceptance
anomalies.loreDesc: Shinichi comes to genuinely care about Migi as a partner rather than a parasite infection.
anomalies.systemDesc: Host acceptance edge that transforms adversarial relationship into cooperative symbiosis.

causalEvents.name: Migi's Partial Takeover
causalEvents.trigger: Parasite attempts to consume Shinichi's brain but only reaches his hand
causalEvents.consequence: Forced co-existence creates a symbiotic partnership that neither organism planned.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The invasion's failure becomes humanity's only hope.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Partial invasion success that produces a co-governance anomaly.

causalEvents.name: First Parasite Kill
causalEvents.trigger: Shinichi and Migi kill their first parasite together
causalEvents.consequence: Establishes the partnership dynamic and gives Shinichi a reason to keep fighting.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The moment Shinichi realizes he and Migi are actually effective as a team.

causalEvents.name: Gotou's True Nature Revealed
causalEvents.trigger: The human-looking student is revealed to be fully parasite-controlled
causalEvents.consequence: Demonstrates that complete infiltration looks exactly like normal human behavior.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The invasion is already won at the social level—humans just don't know it.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Perfect infiltration node that invalidates behavioral detection methods.

causalEvents.name: Final Confrontation with the Core
causalEvents.trigger: Shinichi and Migi face the parasite reproductive core
causalEvents.consequence: The climax reveals the full scale of the invasion and Migi's ultimate choice.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Humanity's survival depends on a boy and his alien partner.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Terminal conflict node where co-existence architecture is tested against species-level threat.

systemQuestions.question: Why is Migi different from other parasites?
systemQuestions.answer: Prolonged contact with Shinichi's human brain chemistry created emotional development that the parasite design doesn't account for. Migi learns to care because living with a human taught him what emotions are.

systemQuestions.question: Why does the parasite invasion work so well?
systemQuestions.answer: Each parasite type has a specific role—infiltrators blend in, combat specialists handle threats, the Core manages reproduction. It's a designed invasion architecture with no wasted units.

systemQuestions.question: Why is Shinichi the only one who can detect infiltrators?
systemQuestions.answer: Living with a parasite gives him inside knowledge of how they think and behave. He knows what parasite thoughts feel like, so he can spot behavioral anomalies no one else can.

systemQuestions.question: What makes Gotou the most dangerous parasite?
systemQuestions.answer: He has a perfect human appearance with full parasite combat capability—but no emotions. He's what the parasites think humans should be: perfectly efficient organisms without the mess of actual feelings.

systemQuestions.question: Why does affinity-matrix work for Parasyte?
systemQuestions.answer: Shinichi and Migi are two distinct nodes sharing one substrate—each with their own abilities and identity, occasionally combining for greater effect but mostly operating independently within the same body.

hero.microHook: Two minds are better than one, unless they disagree on saving the world.
hero.thesis: A human and parasite co-govern one body—Shinichi decides, Migi fights. Neither could survive alone.
